title: The empty seat rule
summary: Two friends wait at a clinic in Toronto and argue about the silent rule of leaving an empty seat between strangers.

body.speaker: Diego
body.text: Look at this waiting room. There are twenty seats, but everyone sits far apart. Why?

body.speaker: Amara
body.text: It is the empty seat rule. People leave one space between strangers. It is polite here.

body.speaker: Diego
body.text: But the room is filling up. Soon someone will have to sit in the middle.

body.speaker: Amara
body.text: Yes, and that is the hard part. Watch. New people always look for two empty seats together first.

body.speaker: Amara
body.text: Then they stand by the wall. They will stand instead of sitting next to someone.

body.speaker: Diego
body.text: That is strange. The seat is free. Sitting is more comfortable. In my town, we just sit down and say hello.

body.speaker: Amara
body.text: Here, a stranger is like a small wall of glass. You can see through it, but you do not break it.

body.speaker: Diego
body.text: But sometimes the wall is good, no? When I am sick and tired, I do not want to talk.

body.speaker: Amara
body.text: True. The empty seat gives you rest. It says, 'You can be quiet. Nobody will bother you.'

body.speaker: Diego
body.text: So it is not cold. It is a kind of gift.

body.speaker: Amara
body.text: Maybe both. Look — that man just sat in the middle seat. Everyone is looking at him.

body.speaker: Diego
body.text: He looks calm. Perhaps he is new here, like me.

body.speaker: Amara
body.text: Or perhaps he is the bravest person in the room.

annotations.meaningVi: quy tắc ghế trống (luật ngầm để một ghế trống giữa người lạ)
thoải mái hơn (so sánh hơn của comfortable)
một kiểu món quà, một dạng quà tặng
người dũng cảm nhất (so sánh nhất của brave)

grammarNotes.title: will for prediction
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'will + động từ' để dự đoán điều sắp xảy ra: 'Soon someone will have to sit in the middle.' / 'They will stand instead of sitting.'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'will + verb' to predict the near future: 'Soon someone will have to sit in the middle.' and 'They will stand instead of sitting.'

grammarNotes.title: Superlative adjectives
grammarNotes.bodyVi: So sánh nhất thêm '-est' hoặc 'the most': brave → the bravest. 'Perhaps he is the bravest person in the room.'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Superlatives use '-est' or 'the most': brave → the bravest. 'Perhaps he is the bravest person in the room.'

translationVi:
Diego: Nhìn phòng chờ này xem. Có hai mươi cái ghế, nhưng ai cũng ngồi cách xa nhau. Tại sao vậy?
Amara: Đó là quy tắc ghế trống. Người ta chừa một khoảng giữa những người lạ. Ở đây như thế là lịch sự.
Diego: Nhưng phòng đang đầy dần. Chẳng mấy chốc sẽ có người phải ngồi vào giữa.
Amara: Đúng vậy, và đó là phần khó. Xem này. Người mới đến luôn tìm hai ghế trống cạnh nhau trước.
Diego: Còn nếu không có thì sao?
Amara: Thì họ đứng dựa tường. Họ thà đứng còn hơn ngồi cạnh người khác.
Diego: Lạ thật. Ghế đang trống mà. Ngồi thì thoải mái hơn. Ở quê tôi, chúng tôi cứ ngồi xuống và chào nhau.
Amara: Ở đây, một người lạ giống như một bức tường kính nhỏ. Bạn nhìn xuyên qua được, nhưng bạn không phá nó.
Diego: Nhưng đôi khi bức tường đó cũng tốt mà, đúng không? Khi tôi ốm và mệt, tôi không muốn nói chuyện.
Amara: Đúng vậy. Cái ghế trống cho bạn nghỉ ngơi. Nó nói: 'Bạn cứ yên lặng đi. Sẽ không ai làm phiền bạn.'
Diego: Vậy nó không lạnh lùng. Nó là một kiểu món quà.
Amara: Có lẽ là cả hai. Nhìn kìa — người đàn ông đó vừa ngồi vào ghế giữa. Mọi người đang nhìn anh ta.
Diego: Anh ta trông bình thản. Có lẽ anh ta mới đến, giống tôi.
Amara: Hoặc có lẽ anh ta là người dũng cảm nhất trong phòng.

questions.prompt: How many seats are in the waiting room?
questions.explanation: 'There are twenty seats, but everyone sits far apart.'

options: You must pay for two seats
People leave one space between strangers
questions.explanation: 'People leave one space between strangers. It is polite here.'
questions.hint: Amara explains it in her first line.

questions.prompt: What do new people look for first?
questions.explanation: 'New people always look for two empty seats together first.'
questions.hint: Look at what Amara says new people do.

questions.prompt: What do people do if there are no two seats together?
questions.hint: Look for what Amara says about standing.

questions.prompt: How is it different in Diego's town?
options: People just sit down and say hello
questions.explanation: 'In my town, we just sit down and say hello.'
questions.hint: Diego compares his town to here.

questions.prompt: What does Amara compare a stranger to?
questions.explanation: 'a stranger is like a small wall of glass. You can see through it, but you do not break it.'
questions.hint: Amara uses a picture, or comparison.

questions.prompt: When does Diego think the empty seat is good?
options: When he is sick and tired and does not want to talk
questions.explanation: 'When I am sick and tired, I do not want to talk.'
questions.hint: Diego changes his mind a little here.

questions.prompt: According to Amara, what does the empty seat 'say'?
options: 'You can be quiet. Nobody will bother you.'
questions.explanation: 'It says, "You can be quiet. Nobody will bother you."'
questions.hint: Amara gives the seat a voice.

questions.prompt: How does Diego finally see the empty seat?
questions.explanation: 'So it is not cold. It is a kind of gift.'
questions.hint: Look near the end of the dialogue.

questions.prompt: What happens when the man sits in the middle seat?
questions.explanation: 'that man just sat in the middle seat. Everyone is looking at him.'
questions.hint: Look at Amara's line about the man.

questions.prompt: What two ideas does Amara give about the man at the very end?
options: He is rich, or he is poor
He is sick, or he is a doctor
He is late, or he is early
He is new here, or he is the bravest person in the room
questions.explanation: Diego says he may be new; Amara adds 'Or perhaps he is the bravest person in the room.'
questions.hint: Read the last two lines together.

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of this dialogue?
options: A silent social rule can be both cold and kind at the same time
Waiting rooms should have more seats
questions.explanation: The empty seat protects rest but also keeps people apart — 'Maybe both.'
questions.hint: Think about how Diego's view changes through the talk.

Amara: It is the empty seat {{b2}}. People leave one space between strangers. It is {{b3}} here.

Diego: But sometimes the wall is good, no? When I am sick and tired, I do not want to talk.
Amara: True. The empty seat gives you {{b9}}. It says, 'You can be quiet. Nobody will {{b10}} you.'

criticalThinkingQuestion: Amara says a stranger is 'like a small wall of glass' that you see through but do not break. Is the empty seat between strangers a polite gift of peace, or a quiet sign that a city teaches people to stay apart?
